lede: A look at March 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Mitt Romney and Liza Minnelli, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United States
events.desc: The United States suspends travel from Europe due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

events.title: 2014 East Harlem gas explosion
events.desc: A gas explosion in the New York City neighborhood of East Harlem kills eight and injures 70 others.

events.title: North Korea
events.desc: North Korea announces that it will withdraw from the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons and refuses to allow inspectors access to its nuclear sites.

events.title: Tim Berners-Lee
events.desc: Tim Berners-Lee submits his proposal to CERN for an information management system, which subsequently develops into the World Wide Web.

events.title: Suharto
events.desc: Suharto takes power from Sukarno when the Provisional People's Consultative Assembly inaugurate him as Acting President of Indonesia.

events.title: Cold War
events.desc: Cold War: The Truman Doctrine is proclaimed to help stem the spread of Communism.

events.title: Mahatma Gandhi
events.desc: Mahatma Gandhi begins the Salt March, a 200-mile (320 km) march to the sea to protest the British monopoly on salt in India.

events.title: California
events.desc: In California, the St. Francis Dam fails; the resulting floods kill 431 people.

events.title: Australia
events.desc: The future capital of Australia is officially named Canberra.

events.title: Ignatius of Loyola
events.desc: Ignatius of Loyola and Francis Xavier, founders of the Society of Jesus, are canonized by the Roman Catholic Church.

births.name: Mitt Romney
births.line: American businessman and politician, 70th Governor of Massachusetts

deaths.text: Terry Pratchett, English journalist, author, and screenwriter
Sun Yat-sen, Chinese physician and politician, 1st President of the Republic of China
Pope Gregory I, pope of the Catholic Church

observances.text: Christian feast day: Bernard of Carinola (or of Capua)
Christian feast day: Gorgonius, Peter Cubicularius and Dorotheus of Nicomedia
